trade.summary: Growing bilateral trade. Turchia exports machinery, vehicles, textiles, and construction materials to Sudafrica; Sudafrica exports minerals, precious metals, chemicals, and agricultural products to Turchia. Turchia's Africa expansion strategy has targeted Sudafrica as a key partner.

diplomatic.summary: Turchia and Sudafrica have developed increasingly warm bilateral relations as part of Turchia's Africa outreach strategy. Both countries are significant regional powers with G20 membership. Turchia opened additional embassies and consulates in Africa. Sudafrica and Turchia have some convergent positions in multilateral forums on global governance reform and climate finance.

military.summary: Limited but growing defence ties. Turchia has been active in selling its Bayraktar TB2 drones to African countries and Sudafrica has expressed interest. Some defence industry cooperation discussions. No formal alliance.

financial.summary: Growing Turkish business investment in Sudafrica. Turkish construction companies have won contracts in Sudafrica. Turkish Airlines operates routes connecting the two markets. Limited Sudafrican investment in Turchia.

energy.summary: Limited direct bilateral energy trade but growing interest. Sudafrica's coal exports flow to global markets including Turchia. Turchia is interested in Sudafrican minerals for energy transition supply chains. Some cooperation in renewable energy technology transfer.

scientific.summary: Limited but growing scientific collaboration. Some academic exchanges under bilateral agreements. Turchia's TUBITAK and Sudafrican research councils have explored partnership frameworks. Cooperation in agricultural and environmental sciences.
